heading: About the vector Type
body:
Your index definition's `vector` field must contain an array of numbers of
*one* of the following types:

- BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) `double`

- BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) [BinData](manual/reference/method/BinData) `vector`
  subtype `float32`

- BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) [BinData](manual/reference/method/BinData) `vector`
  subtype `int1`

- BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) [BinData](manual/reference/method/BinData) `vector`
  subtype `int8`

> **Note:** To learn more about generating BSON (Binary Javascript Object Notation) [BinData](manual/reference/method/BinData) vectors with subtype `float32`
> `int1` or `int8` for your data, see
> [How to Ingest Pre-Quantized Vectors](avs-bindata-vector-subtype).

You must index the vector field as the `vector` type inside the
`fields` array.

The following syntax defines the `vector` field type:

```json
{
  "fields":[
    {
      "type": "vector",
      "path": <field-to-index>,
      "numDimensions": <number-of-dimensions>,
      "similarity": "euclidean | cosine | dotProduct",
      "quantization": "none | scalar | binary",
      "hnswOptions": {
        "maxEdges": <number-of-connected-neighbors>,
        "numEdgeCandidates": <number-of-nearest-neighbors>
      }
    },
    ...
  ]
}
```
